comments.body: To use Codex here, [create an environment for this repo](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/settings/environments).

Codex Review: Didn't find any major issues. :tada:
ℹ️ About Codex in GitHub
[Your team has set up Codex to review pull requests in this repo](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/settings/general). Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. Codex can also answer questions or update the PR. Try commenting "@codex address that feedback".

Verification performed locally on this branch (`voice-toggle-defaults`) with no code changes after the PR commit.

Local stack used:
- Frontend dev server: `http://localhost:5173`
- FastAPI: `http://127.0.0.1:8000`
- OpenCode provider: `http://127.0.0.1:4096`
- LiveKit: `ws://127.0.0.1:7880`
- LiveKit worker restarted with `LIVEKIT_URL=ws://127.0.0.1:7880` and `FRIDAY_API_BASE_URL=http://127.0.0.1:8000`

Static/build checks:
- `cd web && npm run typecheck` passed.
- `uv run mypy` passed: `Success: no issues found in 51 source files`.
- `cd web && npm run build` passed. Vite still reports the existing large chunk warning.

Rendered browser verification:
- Used `agent-browser` desktop viewport `1440x1000` to open the app, open the new-session modal, and verify the directory field renders as `/home/sahil/projects`.
- In the same modal, switched harness to `Codex` and verified the model dropdown selected `OpenAI / GPT-5.5 Codex` by default.
- Verified the Codex model dropdown includes `OpenAI / GPT-5.3 Codex Spark`.
- Created a real Codex session and confirmed the room rendered with model `openai/gpt-5.5`, client connected, agent ready, and the main voice button shown as `Start`.
- Used a temporary Playwright check with fake microphone media to exercise the actual voice room control: clicked `Start`, observed the button become `Send` and the UI enter listening state, clicked `Send`, and observed the UI move into sending/transcribing state with the button disabled as `Sending...` during the end-turn RPC.
- Used `agent-browser` mobile viewport `390x844` against the same session and verified the room renders cleanly with the `Start` button visible and tappable, no obvious overlap/clipping in the voice controls, and the transcript/composer below.

Screenshot artifacts from local verification:
- `/tmp/friday-toggle-verification-desktop.png`
- `/tmp/friday-toggle-mobile.png`

Self-test attempts and blockers:
- `cd web && npm run friday:self-test -- --headless --output /tmp/friday-self-tests --directory /home/sahil/projects --hold-ms 2000 --wait-after-release-ms 45000 --task 'Reply with exactly: Friday voice self test passed.'` could not run fully because this Linux machine does not have the macOS `say` command that the script currently requires to synthesize speech.
- `cd web && npm run friday:text-test -- --headless --output /tmp/friday-self-tests --directory /home/sahil/projects --text-channel direct --wait-after-release-ms 45000 --task 'Reply with exactly: Friday direct text self test passed.'` reached the app, created a session, connected LiveKit, submitted the turn, and then failed at the provider with an OpenRouter credit/max-token limit error. I am not treating that as a regression in this PR because the changed surface is the voice control behavior, default directory, and Codex model defaults.

Codex Review: Didn't find any major issues. :tada:
ℹ️ About Codex in GitHub
[Your team has set up Codex to review pull requests in this repo](https://chatgpt.com/codex/cloud/settings/general). Reviews are triggered when you - Open a pull request for review - Mark a draft as ready - Comment "@codex review". If Codex has suggestions, it will comment; otherwise it will react with 👍. Codex can also answer questions or update the PR. Try commenting "@codex address that feedback".
